title: How to manage a transport issue (lost, stolen, damaged parcel) ?
bodyHtml:
In cases of disputes related to shipping, it’s your sole responsibility to file a claim with your shipping provider and provide the necessary documents.

To streamline the customer experience, Back Market has simplified the process for customers by:

  • Allowing them 14 days to report cases of damage, empty packages, or incorrect / missing products. Don’t worry, it is only to cover the edge cases where customers are not present the days following the delivery. We do not communicate this change proactively to the customers.
  • Considering a package lost after 6 days without tracking updates, triggering a refund or the seller sending a new package 
  • No longer requiring the certificate of non-receipt (which will no longer be sent to you)


This doesn’t mean that we’re relaxing our fraud detection though. We’ve increased detection methods with additional customer verification if we suspect fraud.


Delivered not received

If the shipping provider rejects your claim due to the failure to provide the required documents, you may request compensation from the Seller Support Center, provided that you:

  • Submit your compensation request within 30 days after the refund date (the shipment date applies if there is not delivery date).
  • Submit a request to the Seller Support Center by categorizing the request as "Non-Backship shipping or delivery".
  • Provide all information and documents requested by Back Market agents.
  • Prove that the shipping provider/insurer rejected your claim due to a lack of required documents, which should have been provided by Back Market.
  • Show that you have ad valorem insurance for the disputed parcel.

If you’re unable to prove that you have ad valorem insurance, compensation will be limited to the legal maximum. In any case, compensation will be capped at 400 € per parcel.

This compensation offered by Back Market doesn’t apply to cases of loss or damage for which the shipping provider is responsible.


Damage, incorrect / missing product

Please note that if the shipping provider rejects your claim because the customer reported the case after 3 days, you may request compensation from the Seller Support Center, provided that you:

  • Submit your compensation request within 30 days after the delivery date (the shipment date applies if there is not delivery date).
  • Submit a request to the Seller Support Center by categorizing the request as "Non-Backship shipping or delivery".
  • Provide all information and documents requested by Back Market agents.
  • Prove that the shipping provider/insurer rejected your claim because of the exceeded deadline. 

 

Big investments to detect fraud and prevent fraudsters

Here’s how we’re investing in fraud detection before it impacts your business: 

  • A task force of about 100 people to handle shipping incidents and identify potential fraud cases.
  • An algorithm based on automatic feedback from our anti-fraud service providers.
  • Scoring based on the type and order history of customers allowing us to better assess risk and be more cautious with high-value orders.
  • Additional identity verifications with photo IDs, Liveness detection and AI algorithms.

If fraud is suspected, we’ll ask the customer for a police report and live identity test before refunding the customer. 

We’re closely monitoring the delivery incident rate and have not seen an increase in refund requests since the launch of this new process. 

If you suspect fraud with one of your shipped orders, submit a request to the Seller Support Center so we can analyze the case. Note that parcels must be insured for you to be refunded.

If your shipping providers aren’t performing, explore our BackShip services.
